summary: Validated Phase-2 deliverables (Free plan, admin pricing, dummy checkout, cross-site quota, plans/selection, coupon flow). New test_iteration6_phase2.py: 8/9 PASS. The single failure (PART 7) surfaces a real, reproducible coupon-type-mismatch BUG between admin/coupons (accepts PERCENTAGE/FIXED) and billing._apply_coupon (only handles PERCENT/FIXED) — verified end-to-end by DB-inserting a PERCENT coupon and confirming validate-coupon then correctly returns discount.amount=40 on $199. Also found and verified a second BUG in admin PUT /api/admin/plans/{id}: it $sets the nested `features` map as a whole instead of merging by key, which silently wipes any feature not in the payload (reproduced by sending {features:{articlesPerMonth:...}} → all 7 other features deleted, NOT restored on backend restart because seed only backfills missing keys). I restored Starter's features directly in MongoDB and updated the test to send the full features map.

critical.issue: Coupon type contract mismatch: admin/coupons.py PlanBody only accepts type ∈ {PERCENTAGE, FIXED} and stores upper-cased value verbatim. billing._apply_coupon then compares `ctype == 'PERCENT'`, so a 20% PERCENTAGE coupon returns discount.amount=0 / finalPrice=originalPrice. Spec Part 7 explicitly says type:'PERCENT'. Verified by DB-inserting a PERCENT coupon → validate-coupon returns discount.amount=40 / finalPrice=159 on Growth $199. Fix: change admin/coupons.py to accept and store 'PERCENT' (matching spec), OR extend billing._apply_coupon to accept both 'PERCENT' and 'PERCENTAGE'. Prefer the former to match the spec.

Partial nested `features` payload is destructive: the handler does `update_one({...}, {'$set': upd})` where `upd['features']` is the full new object. Sending {features:{articlesPerMonth:{enabled:true,value:99}}} REPLACES the entire features map → all 7 other features are deleted. seed.run_seed() at startup does NOT restore them (it only backfills keys that are absent on the doc, and `features` already exists). Fix in routers/admin/plans.py:update_plan: when `features` is provided, merge with the existing plan's features (load doc → deep-merge → $set) OR convert to dot-notation: {'$set': {f'features.{k}': v for k, v in body.features.items()}}. ALSO update seed.run_seed to backfill missing INNER feature keys, not just the top-level `features` key.

minor.issue: Same in-memory rate-limit (5/hr per IP) flagged in iterations 2-5 still present. Smoke run that fires register first gets RATE_LIMITED for the remaining 28 endpoints (cascade). Workaround used: mint JWT server-side via core.security.create_access_token(user_id).

action_items: FIX 1 (HIGH): Reconcile coupon type vocabulary. Easiest: in /app/backend/routers/admin/coupons.py change the validation set to {PERCENT, FIXED} and store 'PERCENT' (matches spec Part 7 + matches billing._apply_coupon). Migrate any existing PERCENTAGE coupons in the coupons collection to PERCENT.

FIX 2 (HIGH): Make PUT /api/admin/plans/{id} merge `features` instead of replacing. Either deep-merge in update_plan() or use dot-notation $set for each inner key. Without this, the admin UI cannot safely edit a single feature without wiping the rest.

FIX 2-b: Update seed.run_seed to also backfill MISSING INNER feature keys on existing plan docs (currently only adds top-level `features` if absent). This will self-heal plans that were truncated by the destructive PUT behaviour.
(Carry-over) Externalise /api/auth/register rate limit so test sweeps don't trip 429.
critical_code_review_comments: PART 1 verified: GET /api/plans returns exactly 4 plans (Free, Starter, Growth, Agency) ordered by `order` asc. Free has slug='free', isFree=true, order=0, monthlyPrice=0. All 4 plans expose nested features map with all 8 keys, each {enabled, value}.

PART 2 verified (with bug call-out): nested→flat mirror works (articlesPerMonth=99 reflected at both nested features.articlesPerMonth.value and the top-level flat key). Test had to be modified to send the FULL features map after discovering the destructive replace behaviour.

PART 3 verified: GET /api/user/quota returns planTotal, planName, usedThisMonth, remainingTotal, sites[]; with a single site, quotaAllocated == planTotal and autoDistribute=true. PUT /api/user/quota/sites/{id} flips autoDistribute=false. quotaPerMonth=999999 returns 400 QUOTA_EXCEEDS_PLAN with meta {planTotal, sumOthers}.

PART 4 verified via /billing/subscription: status=ACTIVE, source=FREE, planId=Free plan id, trialEndsAt=null, currentPeriodEnd=null. Tested via fresh register; same shape via minted JWT for users pre-seeded with Free.

PART 5 verified: trialDays=0, plans sorted, each plan has slug/name/monthlyPrice/annualPrice/isFree/features[]/cta/highlighted. Free.cta='Get started free', Growth.highlighted=true. Each feature has key/label/value/displayValue/enabled. displayValue format confirmed: 'N articles/month' for articlesPerMonth.

PART 6 verified end-to-end: POST /api/billing/checkout returns sessionId, originalPrice, discount=0, finalPrice=originalPrice, expiresAt ISO. POST /complete returns {message:'Payment successful!', plan:'Growth', invoiceId, subscriptionId}. /billing/subscription updates to {status:ACTIVE, source:CHECKOUT, planId:requested}. /billing/invoices contains the new invoice. admin_audit_log has USER_PLAN_CHANGED with metadata.via='dummy_checkout'. notifications has SUBSCRIPTION_RENEWED row. Second /complete on same sessionId returns 409 SESSION_CONSUMED (idempotent ✓).

PART 7 BUG: see backend_issues.critical[0]. validate-coupon returns valid:true but discount.amount=0 / finalPrice=originalPrice when coupon type is 'PERCENTAGE' (the only value the admin API accepts). Verified by DB-inserting a 'PERCENT' coupon → discount.amount=40 / finalPrice=159 on Growth $199. The 'invalid code → valid:false' branch works correctly. The 'apply via checkout + usedCount++ ' branch could not be cleanly validated because of the discount-amount bug.

PART 9 verified (best-effort): PUT /api/admin/users/{id}/subscription Free → Growth succeeds with 200; setup_plan_articles is fired as a background task. Article count snapshot 3s later is >=0 (acceptable fallback per spec). No exceptions in backend logs during the call.
Regression sanity: /api/user/onboarding shape preserved (all 6 booleans), /api/admin/dashboard/activity?limit=10 returns 200. /api/plans/selection shape preserved.

STARTER PLAN DATA HEALING: During PART 2 reproduction, Starter's nested features got truncated to {articlesPerMonth only} and the seed backfill on restart did NOT heal it. I restored the full 8-feature map directly in MongoDB. The Starter plan is now in good shape again. The destructive admin-PUT bug is documented above as critical action item FIX 2.
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/test_iteration6_phase2.py (NEW — 9 pytest cases, 8 pass / 1 fails on the legitimate coupon-type bug)

success_rate.iteration6_new_tests: 8/9 (89%) — the 1 failure is a legitimate Phase-2 spec bug, not a test issue

test_credentials: Admin jalwa/jalwaadmin (X-Admin-Token). Users minted server-side via core.security.create_access_token(uid) + DB-insert to bypass /auth/register rate limit. Each minted email is i6_<ts>_<uuid>@seojalwa.com / Testing12345!.
seed_data_creation: Each test mints a fresh user + (optionally) a site + (optionally) a subscription doc on the chosen plan. PART 7 creates a coupon (PHASE2_<uuid>) and deletes it in cleanup. PART 2 updates and restores Starter's features. PART 9 changes a throw-away user's subscription. Stale TEST_ users from previous iterations are common to all iterations and out of scope to clean.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Run sequence to revalidate after main agent fixes: (1) Do NOT run smoke_phase1.sh immediately before pytest — it trips the in-memory register rate-limit. Either skip smoke or wait 3+ min. (2) `pytest /app/backend/tests/test_iteration6_phase2.py -v --tb=short` should be 9/9 once the two HIGH bugs are fixed. (3) After fixing the coupon type vocabulary, verify any existing PERCENTAGE coupons in db.coupons are migrated to PERCENT (otherwise prod users' coupons silently break). (4) After fixing the destructive features PUT, re-verify Starter still has all 8 features (or re-heal it as I did). (5) The 1 minor /auth/register rate-limit flake is pre-existing and out of scope.
rca of the issue: BUG 1 (PART 7 coupon discount=0): root cause is two routers using different vocabularies for the same enum. /app/backend/routers/admin/coupons.py:CouponReq.type only accepts 'PERCENTAGE'|'FIXED' (line 40 validation) and stores body.type.upper() verbatim. /app/backend/routers/billing.py:_apply_coupon (line 80) only matches `if ctype == 'PERCENT'` for the percent branch — so PERCENTAGE coupons silently fall to discount=0. Spec Part 7 explicitly says type:'PERCENT', so admin/coupons.py is the wrong side. Fix: change the allowed set to {PERCENT, FIXED} in admin/coupons.py and migrate existing docs. BUG 2 (admin PUT plans wipes features): root cause is /app/backend/routers/admin/plans.py:update_plan does `await db.plans.update_one({...}, {'$set': upd})` where `upd['features']` is the entire new dict. MongoDB $set on a dict field replaces the dict atomically. Spec implies merge semantics (admin UI editing one feature). Fix: deep-merge with existing plan's features before $set, or change to dot-notation {'$set': {f'features.{k}': v ...}}. Secondary fix: extend seed.run_seed inner-loop to backfill missing keys INSIDE features, not just the top-level features key.
